formulas.name: Content
formulas.formula:
Here is the complete formula sheet for CBSE Class 12 Mathematics - Vector Algebra:

[
  {
    "name": "Position Vector",
    "formula": "\\vec{a} = x\\hat{i} + y\\hat{j} + z\\hat{k}",
    "variables": "x, y, z are components of the vector. \\hat{i}, \\hat{j}, \\hat{k} are unit vectors along x, y, z axes.",
    "units": "No units",
    "notes": ""
  },
  {
    "name": "Magnitude of a Vector",
    "formula": "|\vec{a}| = \sqrt{x^2 + y^2 + z^2}",
    "variables": "x, y, z are components of the vector.",
    "units": "Meters (m)",
    "notes": ""
  },
  {
    "name": "Unit Vector",
    "formula": "\\hat{a} = \frac{\vec{a}}{|\vec{a}|}",
    "variables": "\\vec{a} is a given vector.",
    "units": "No units",
    "notes": ""
  },
  {
    "name": "Vector Addition",
    "formula": "\\vec{a} + \\vec{b} = (a_1 + b_1)\\hat{i} + (a_2 + b_2)\\hat{j} + (a_3 + b_3)\\hat{k}",
    "variables": "\\vec{a} = a_1\\hat{i} + a_2\\hat{j} + a_3\\hat{k}, \\vec{b} = b_1\\hat{i} + b_2\\hat{j} + b_3\\hat{k}",
    "units": "No units",
    "notes": ""
  },
  {
    "name": "Scalar Multiplication",
    "formula": "c\vec{a} = c(a_1\\hat{i} + a_2\\hat{j} + a_3\\hat{k}) = ca_1\\hat{i} + ca_2\\hat{j} + ca_3\\hat{k}",
    "variables": "c is a scalar and \\vec{a} is a vector.",
    "units": "No units",
    "notes": ""
  },
  {
    "name": "Dot Product",
    "formula": "\\vec{a} \cdot \\vec{b} = a_1b_1 + a_2b_2 + a_3b_3",
    "variables": "\\vec{a} = a_1\\hat{i} + a_2\\hat{j} + a_3\\hat{k}, \\vec{b} = b_1\\hat{i} + b_2\\hat{j} + b_3\\hat{k}",
    "units": "No units",
    "notes": ""
  },
  {
    "name": "Cross Product",
    "formula": "\\vec{a} \times \\vec{b} = \begin{vmatrix} \hat{i} & \hat{j} & \hat{k} \\ a_1 & a_2 & a_3 \\ b_1 & b_2 & b_3 \end{vmatrix}",
    "variables": "\\vec{a} = a_1\\hat{i} + a_2\\hat{j} + a_3\\hat{k}, \\vec{b} = b_1\\hat{i} + b_2\\hat{j} + b_3\\hat{k}",
    "units": "No units",
    "notes": ""
  },
  {
    "name": "Vector Triple Product",
    "formula": "\\vec{a} \times (\vec{b} \times \vec{c}) = \vec{b}(\vec{a} \cdot \vec{c}) - \vec{c}(\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b})",
    "variables": "\\vec{a}, \\vec{b}, \\vec{c} are vectors.",
    "units": "No units",
    "notes": ""
  },
  {
    "name": "Parallelogram Law of Vector Addition",
    "formula": "\\vec{a} + \\vec{b} = \\vec{c} \\iff \\text{Area of parallelogram} = |\vec{c}|^2 = |\vec{a}|^2 + |\vec{b}|^2 + 2\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}",
    "variables": "\\vec{a}, \\vec{b}, \\vec{c} are vectors.",
    "units": "No units",
    "notes": ""
  },
  {
    "name": "Triangle Law of Vector Addition",
    "formula": "\\vec{a} + \\vec{b} = \\vec{c} \\iff |\vec{c}|^2 = |\vec{a}|^2 + |\vec{b}|^2 - 2\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}",
    "variables": "\\vec{a}, \\vec{b}, \\vec{c} are vectors.",
    "units": "No units",
    "notes": ""
  }
]
